text: You have a right to action, never to its fruits. Let not the fruits of action be your motive, nor your attachment to inaction.
When meditation is mastered, the mind is unwavering like the flame of a lamp in a windless place.
Lift yourself by yourself; do not let yourself sink. For the self is its own friend, and the self is its own enemy.
The disciplined mind brings wisdom; the restless mind is scattered.
Better is one’s own duty, though imperfect, than the duty of another well performed.
From whatever you set your heart on, you will be bound; be free from all pairs of opposites and be steady in yoga.
He who sees inaction in action and action in inaction—he is wise among people.
The senses roam, but bring them back to the self, like horses trained by a charioteer.
If I give up now, I’ll regret it for the rest of my life.
I don’t have to win, but I won’t run away.
Power isn’t determined by your size, but the size of your heart and dreams.
Being alone is painful. But I’ll get stronger.
I decided to be Pirate King. I don’t care if I die for it.
You want to keep everyone safe? Then get stronger.
It’s not the goal that matters—it’s the discipline to keep moving toward it.
A genius? No, I’m not. I just work hard.
The stronger you get, the heavier your responsibilities become.
If you don’t like your fate, don’t accept it. Instead, have the courage to change it.
Pain is your ally—it tells you you’re still in the fight.
Train until the weak voice in your head goes quiet.
The future belongs to those who prepare in the dark while others sleep.
You do not rise to the level of your goals. You fall to the level of your systems.
Small daily improvements are the key to staggering long-term results.
Patience is not passive waiting. Patience is active endurance.
Compound interest is the eighth wonder of the world. Consistency unlocks it.
The best time to plant a tree was twenty years ago. The second best time is now.
Discipline is remembering what you want.
Amateurs wait for motivation. Professionals build systems.
You can’t cheat the grind—it knows how much you’ve invested.
Self-respect is built in small kept promises to yourself.
The cost of discipline is always less than the price of regret.
Do the hard thing first. Freedom follows execution.
